displayTitle: Pick Your Particles, Time Your Shot
kicker: MT LEVEL I // WET & DRY PARTICLES

beats.narration: The magnetizing gear builds the field, but particles are the detector. Choosing and applying them right makes or breaks the test.

payload.title: WET OR DRY
payload.sub: Particles are the detector — choose and apply them right.

beats.narration: Good particles share four traits: high permeability, low retentivity so they don't clump, a controlled size and shape blend, and strong contrast against the surface.

payload.heading: Four traits of a good particle

beats.narration: Dry powder tumbles over rough, hot work, some rated to roughly six hundred degrees Fahrenheit. The far finer wet bath catches fine, shallow cracks.

payload.heading: Dry for rough and hot — wet for fine
payload.note: DRY: ROUGH & HOT · WET: FINE, SHALLOW CRACKS

beats.narration: The continuous method: indications form while current flows. Dry: dust a light cloud, clear excess with gentle air, all under power. Wet: flood, divert, shoot.
So apply particles like the precision detection medium they are. Particles are cheap. Missed cracks are not.

payload.sub: Apply them like the precision detection medium they are.
